conversation.message: How have you been feeling lately?
Honestly, I’ve been really down. I try to focus on the day ahead, but the fatigue just wears me out.
You mentioned the fatigue wears you out—how has that been affecting your day-to-day routine?
It makes everything a chore. I used to enjoy simple things like walking the dog, but now it feels like climbing a mountain.
You mentioned you used to enjoy walking the dog—what else has started to feel like a chore?
Cooking. I look at the recipe and just feel like I can't do it. I end up ordering takeout because it's just easier.
When you feel like you can't do something you used to manage, like cooking, how does that make you think about yourself?
I think about how I used to be the one helping others, and now I can barely take care of myself. It's hard to accept that I'm not that person anymore.
You mentioned that things feel pointless - when did that start feeling this strong?
It started when my partner left. We were together for years, and suddenly I was just... alone. I don't know how to be me without him.
When you say nothing matters, what parts of life feel most empty lately?
Everything. I wake up, I exist, I go to bed. I don't have a purpose anymore. I'm just waiting for the day to be over.
How do you see the next few weeks unfolding for you?
I don't know. I just exist until the next thing happens, and I don't think anything big is coming. I'm just stuck in this hole.
How much of this feels like giving up versus feeling exhausted?
It's both. I don't think I can keep doing this, but I don't know how to stop it.
Do you feel like people would be better off without you, or is it more that you feel stuck?
I think they would be better off without me. I'm just a burden, and I don't know how to stop being one.
When you think about being a burden, what does that look like in your day-to-day life?
It's just the small things. I call my friend, and she has to leave work to talk to me, or I need to borrow money from my sister. I'm just draining everyone's energy, and I don't know how to stop it.
How have you been feeling lately?
I have been really down lately. It’s been a few weeks, and I just can’t seem to shake the feeling that I should be doing better by now.
Has that feeling of being down affected your sleep at all?
Yeah, it has. I wake up in the middle of the night and just lie there thinking about all the things I could have done differently.
When you're lying awake at night thinking about those things, does it make it harder to find the motivation to do things you normally enjoy during the day?
Yeah, it does. I just don't have the energy to do much of anything. I feel like I'm just going through the motions right now.
When you think about those things you could have done differently, how does that make you feel about yourself?
It makes me feel like I’m not good enough. Like I should have been better prepared or more proactive, and now I’m just stuck in this rut.
You mentioned that things feel pointless - when did that start feeling this strong?
I think it started a few weeks ago. I realized that I was doing the same things over and over, and it just didn't feel like it was leading anywhere.
When you say nothing matters, what parts of life feel most empty lately?
Work, mostly. I used to enjoy my job, but now it just feels like a chore. I go in, do my tasks, and then go home without really feeling like I've accomplished anything.
When you think about the future, does it feel like things can get better, or does it seem like you'll be stuck in this rut for a while?
I don't know. I just feel so stuck. I look at other people who seem to have their lives together, and I just can't help but wonder what I'm doing wrong.
How do you see the next few weeks unfolding for you?
I don't know. I just see more of the same, to be honest. I keep thinking that eventually things will get better, but so far, they haven't.
How much of this feels like giving up versus feeling exhausted?
Mostly exhausted. I don't want to give up, but I just can't keep pushing forward when it feels like I'm getting nowhere.
Do you feel like people would be better off without you, or is it more that you feel stuck?
It's more that I feel stuck. I know I’m not a bad person, I just... I don't know how to get out of this headspace.
